case: Nesic 2007 - 5% CO2 flow-loop at 60 C
conditions: T 60 C, pCO2 5 bar, pH 5.0, flow 1.0 m/s, 15 mm pipe, Fe2+ 5 ppm, 1 wt% NaCl, no scale, no inhibitor
source: Nesic S., Corrosion Sci. 49 (2007) 4308; Ohio U flow-loop
note: Reference dataset behind FreeCorp/Multicorp calibration. Ported verbatim from austenite co2.ts CO2_VALIDATIONS (nesic-2007-loop).

case: Tengiz (KZ) sour-sweet field - Kahyarian 2017
conditions: T 92 C, pCO2 6.5 bar, pH 4.0, flow 3.5 m/s, 300 mm pipe, Fe2+ 40 ppm, water cut 0.45, H2S 1.4 mol%
source: Kahyarian A., Singer M., Nesic S., J. Nat. Gas Sci. Eng. 41 (2017) 14
note: Sour-sweet field; highest-CR location on flowline. Ported verbatim from austenite co2.ts CO2_VALIDATIONS (kahyarian-tengiz-2017).

case: Top-of-line corrosion - Singer 2017
conditions: T 75 C, pCO2 8 bar, pH 3.8, gas velocity 0.05 m/s, 100 mm pipe, Fe2+ 2 ppm, condensing water, no buffering
source: Singer M., Camacho A., Brown B., Nesic S., Corrosion 73 (2017) 1030
note: Condensing-water TLC; very low Fe2+ + no buffering = aggressive. Ported verbatim from austenite co2.ts CO2_VALIDATIONS (singer-tlc-2017).

case: 13Cr OCTG threshold - Mishina 2014
conditions: T 130 C, pCO2 30 bar, pH 3.8, flow 2.0 m/s, 100 mm pipe, Fe2+ 50 ppm; Cr passivation above ~120 C
source: Mishina K. et al., NACE Paper 4226 (2014)
note: 13Cr CR base ~0.1 mm/y vs CS baseline 5-8x higher. Ported verbatim from austenite co2.ts CO2_VALIDATIONS (mishina-2014-13cr). NACE conference paper; no DOI.

case: HP/HT well @ 175 C, 100 bar CO2 - Nyborg 2007
conditions: T 175 C, pCO2 100 bar, pH 4.5, flow 5.0 m/s, 100 mm pipe, Fe2+ 60 ppm; protective FeCO3 scale
source: Nyborg R., NACE Paper 07522 (2007)
note: High-T FeCO3 scale dominates; without scale CR would be ~15 mm/y. Ported verbatim from austenite co2.ts CO2_VALIDATIONS (nyborg-2007-hpht). NACE conference paper; no DOI.

case: Elgaddafi 2015 - autoclave 80 C, 4.1 bar CO2
conditions: T 80 C, pCO2 0.41 MPa (4.1 bar), 2% NaCl, stagnant, 187 h; steady-state LPR; scale-forming (FeCO3 >60 C); AISI C1045
source: Elgaddafi R. et al., J. Nat. Gas Sci. Eng. 27 (2015) 1620
note: First in-envelope CO2 case sourced via the live library hunt. Steady-state (187 h) LPR rate, text-stated as 'approximately 0.32 mm/y' at 0.41 MPa CO2, 80 C. Scale-forming regime; pH not reported (computed in-situ, no added bicarbonate).

case: Elgaddafi 2015 - autoclave 80 C, 6.2 bar CO2
conditions: T 80 C, pCO2 0.62 MPa (6.2 bar), 2% NaCl, stagnant, 187 h; steady-state LPR; scale-forming; AISI C1045
source: Elgaddafi R. et al., J. Nat. Gas Sci. Eng. 27 (2015) 1620
note: Companion to the 4.1 bar point. Steady-state (187 h) LPR rate, text-stated as 'approximately 0.28 mm/y' at 0.62 MPa CO2, 80 C. Scale-forming; pH computed in-situ.

case: Peng 2024 - L360N blank, CO2-saturated 5% NaCl, 40 C
conditions: T 40 C, CO2-saturated (~0.93 bar pCO2 at 1 atm), 5 wt% NaCl, 72 h weight-loss; L360N carbon steel; blank (no inhibitor)
source: Peng L. et al., Materials 17(5) (2024) 1094
note: In-envelope blank weight-loss CR (Table 1) from an open-access MDPI inhibitor study; 72 h average at 40 C, CO2-saturated 5% NaCl. Explicit uncertainty +/-0.005. pH not reported (computed in-situ). Independent of Elgaddafi; both show bulk active-corrosion models over-predict the multi-day weight-loss rate.

case: Aristia 2019 - St-37 geothermal brine, 70 C, CO2 purged
conditions: T 70 C, CO2 continuous purge (~0.69 bar pCO2 at 1 atm), geothermal brine (1500 mg/L Cl-, 15 mg/L HCO3-), pH 4, 7 d; St-37 (S235JR) carbon steel
source: Aristia G., Hoa L.Q., Bassler R., Materials 12(22) (2019) 3801
note: In-envelope geothermal-brine CO2 rate, text-stated 0.16 mm/y at 70 C; low chloride (1500 mg/L) + 7 d exposure -> scaled/low. pH 4 measured. Open access (MDPI).

case: Tang 2018 - Q235 blank, CO2-saturated 3.5% NaCl, 60 C
conditions: T 60 C, CO2-saturated (~0.8 bar pCO2 at 1 atm), 3.5 wt% NaCl, 72 h weight-loss; Q235 carbon steel; blank (no inhibitor)
source: Tang J. et al., Molecules 23(12) (2018) 3270
note: Blank weight-loss CR from Table 1, reported as 0.345 g/m2/h, converted to 0.385 mm/y (rho 7.85 g/cm3: 1 g/m2/h = 1.116 mm/y). 60 C, CO2-sat 3.5% NaCl, 72 h. pH computed in-situ. Open access.

case: Imidazoline RSC 2019 - Q235 blank, CO2-saturated 2% NaCl, 20 C
conditions: T 20 C (293 K), CO2-saturated (~0.99 bar pCO2 at 1 atm), 2 wt% NaCl, pH 4.11, 72 h weight-loss; Q235 carbon steel; blank (no inhibitor)
source: Corrosion inhibition of mild steel by an imidazoline hydrolysate in CO2-saturated solution, RSC Adv. 9 (2019), Table 1
note: Blank (uninhibited) weight-loss CR from Table 1, reported directly in mm/y at 293 K (20 C). pH 4.11 measured. Lowest-temperature anchor of the open CO2 corpus. Open access (RSC Advances); value verified against the source PDF Table 1.

case: Imidazoline RSC 2019 - Q235 blank, CO2-saturated 2% NaCl, 60 C
conditions: T 60 C (333 K), CO2-saturated (~0.81 bar pCO2 at 1 atm), 2 wt% NaCl, pH 4.11, 72 h weight-loss; Q235 carbon steel; blank (no inhibitor)
source: Corrosion inhibition of mild steel by an imidazoline hydrolysate in CO2-saturated solution, RSC Adv. 9 (2019), Table 1
note: Blank weight-loss CR from Table 1, reported directly in mm/y at 333 K (60 C). pH 4.11 measured. Second independent 60 C point (cf. Tang 2018, different steel/study). Open access (RSC Advances); value verified against the source PDF Table 1.

case: 304 stainless - CPT in ferric chloride
conditions: 10% FeCl3 (ASTM G48 ferric-chloride family); 18Cr-8.6Ni-0.05Mo heat
source: Brigham R.J., Tozer E.W., Corrosion 30 (1974) 161
note: Low-PREN austenitic anchor; pits just above room temperature. Value redistributed in Nyby C. et al., Sci. Data 8:58 (2021) CC-BY (dataset DOI 10.6084/m9.figshare.13038257); primary-source DOI shown.

case: 316L stainless - CPT in ferric chloride
conditions: 10% FeCl3 (ASTM G48 ferric-chloride family); 17Cr-12Ni-2.5Mo
source: Brigham R.J., Tozer E.W., Corrosion 30 (1974) 161
note: Standard 2.5Mo austenitic anchor. Same FeCl3 study as the 304 row (internally consistent pair). Redistributed in Nyby C. et al., Sci. Data 8:58 (2021) CC-BY.

case: 317L stainless - CPT in ferric chloride
conditions: 10% FeCl3 (ASTM G48 ferric-chloride family); 19Cr-14Ni-3.1Mo
source: Brigham R.J., Tozer E.W., Corrosion 30 (1974) 161
note: 3Mo austenitic, between 316L and 904L. Redistributed in Nyby C. et al., Sci. Data 8:58 (2021) CC-BY.

case: 904L (N08904) - CPT in 1 M NaCl
conditions: 1 M NaCl, potentiodynamic electrochemical CPT; 19.8Cr-23.9Ni-4.36Mo-0.055N
source: Sun Y.T., Wang J.M., Jiang Y.M., Li J., Materials and Corrosion 69 (2018) 44
note: High-alloy austenitic anchor. Electrochemical (1 M NaCl) method - higher than a FeCl3 G48 value would be; method noted. Redistributed in Nyby C. et al., Sci. Data 8:58 (2021) CC-BY.

case: 254 SMO (S31254, 6Mo) - CPT in 1 M NaCl
conditions: 1 M NaCl, potentiodynamic electrochemical CPT; 19.8Cr-17.8Ni-5.88Mo-0.19N
source: Sun Y.T., Wang J.M., Jiang Y.M., Li J., Materials and Corrosion 69 (2018) 44
note: 6Mo super-austenitic high-end anchor. Electrochemical (1 M NaCl) method noted. Redistributed in Nyby C. et al., Sci. Data 8:58 (2021) CC-BY.

case: 2205 (S31803/S32205) duplex - CPT in acidified ferric chloride
conditions: Acidified ferric chloride (ASTM G48 family), standard immersion method; 22Cr-5Ni-3.2Mo-0.2N duplex
source: Hoseinpoor M., Momeni M., Moayed M.H., Davoodi A., Corrosion Sci. 80 (2014) 197
note: Standard-method CPT = 40 C (paper also reports 44 C ZRA / 49 C potentiostatic). Duplex mid-range anchor.

case: 254 SMO (S31254, 6Mo) - CPT by ASTM G48 Method E
conditions: ASTM G48 Method E (6% FeCl3 + 1% HCl, ferric-chloride immersion), 24 h; 20Cr-18Ni-6.1Mo-0.2N
source: Outokumpu, 'Testing stainless steel for resistance to pitting corrosion' (2023)
note: On-basis ASTM G48 Method E CPT (65 C) from the alloy's originator (Outokumpu); the same source gives the electrochemical ASTM G150 CPT as 85 C, consistent with the 86.49 C electrochemical row above. First high-PREN (6Mo) point on the G48 basis the correlation predicts. Manufacturer technical source (labelled, stable; no DOI): https://www.outokumpu.com/en/expertise/2023/testing-stainless-steel-for-resistance-to-pitting-corrosion

case: Coatings 2025 - blank, CO2-sat chloride-carbonate brine 60 C (AISI 1018)
conditions: T 60 C, CO2-saturated at 1 atm (pCO2 ~0.8 bar), 30 g/L NaCl + 0.1 g/L NaHCO3 + 0.1 g/L CaCO3, AISI 1018, 72 h mass loss, quiescent; pH computed in-situ
source: Coatings 15(1):93 (2025) - uninhibited (blank) corrosion rate v0 (oleic-acid-imidazoline/CeCl3 inhibitor study)
note: Blank (no-inhibitor) weight-loss rate of AISI 1018 in CO2-saturated simulated oilfield brine (chloride-carbonate). In-envelope (T 60 C, pCO2 ~1 bar). Distinct from existing 60 C NaCl cases (chloride-carbonate brine, higher salinity).

case: Thiophene-imidazoline RSC 2025 - Q235 blank, CO2-sat formation water, 90 C
conditions: T 90 C, pCO2 0.31 bar (CO2-saturated ~1 atm), simulated formation water (3.5% NaCl + 0.06% CaCl2 + 0.03% NaHCO3 + 0.02% MgCl2), pH 4.13, blank (no inhibitor)
source: Jia et al., RSC Adv. (2025), DOI 10.1039/d5ra04201a - inhibitor-free (blank) weight-loss rate (text, Sec. 3.2)
note: Blank Q235 weight-loss, CO2-saturated formation water at 1 atm (pCO2~0.31 bar at 90 C), 22 h, pH 4.13. Text-stated: 'at 90 C, the corrosion rate of carbon steel in the inhibitor-free solution reaches 1.8115 mm/a'. Highest-T CO2 anchor in corpus.

case: 2-phenyl imidazoline AJC 2023 - X65 blank, CO2-sat 3% NaCl, 20 C
conditions: T 20 C, pCO2 0.99 bar (CO2-saturated ~1 atm), 3 wt% NaCl, pH 4.11, blank (no inhibitor)
source: Jiang, Wang et al., Arabian J. Chemistry 16(6):104774 (2023), DOI 10.1016/j.arabjc.2023.104774 - X65 inhibitor-free (blank) weight-loss rate, Table 2 (converted from g/m2/h x1.116)
note: Blank X65 weight-loss, CO2-saturated 3% NaCl at 1 atm, 72 h, pH 4.11 (measured). 2-phenyl imidazoline study Table 2, 0 mg/L row.

case: 2-phenyl imidazoline AJC 2023 - X65 blank, CO2-sat 3% NaCl, 30 C
conditions: T 30 C, pCO2 0.97 bar (CO2-saturated ~1 atm), 3 wt% NaCl, pH 4.11, blank (no inhibitor)
source: Jiang, Wang et al., Arabian J. Chemistry 16(6):104774 (2023), DOI 10.1016/j.arabjc.2023.104774 - X65 inhibitor-free (blank) weight-loss rate, Table 2 (converted from g/m2/h x1.116)
note: Blank X65 weight-loss, CO2-saturated 3% NaCl at 1 atm, 72 h, pH 4.11 (measured). 2-phenyl imidazoline study Table 2, 0 mg/L row.

case: 2-phenyl imidazoline AJC 2023 - X65 blank, CO2-sat 3% NaCl, 40 C
conditions: T 40 C, pCO2 0.94 bar (CO2-saturated ~1 atm), 3 wt% NaCl, pH 4.11, blank (no inhibitor)
source: Jiang, Wang et al., Arabian J. Chemistry 16(6):104774 (2023), DOI 10.1016/j.arabjc.2023.104774 - X65 inhibitor-free (blank) weight-loss rate, Table 2 (converted from g/m2/h x1.116)
note: Blank X65 weight-loss, CO2-saturated 3% NaCl at 1 atm, 72 h, pH 4.11 (measured). 2-phenyl imidazoline study Table 2, 0 mg/L row.

case: 2-phenyl imidazoline AJC 2023 - X65 blank, CO2-sat 3% NaCl, 50 C
conditions: T 50 C, pCO2 0.89 bar (CO2-saturated ~1 atm), 3 wt% NaCl, pH 4.11, blank (no inhibitor)
source: Jiang, Wang et al., Arabian J. Chemistry 16(6):104774 (2023), DOI 10.1016/j.arabjc.2023.104774 - X65 inhibitor-free (blank) weight-loss rate, Table 2 (converted from g/m2/h x1.116)
note: Blank X65 weight-loss, CO2-saturated 3% NaCl at 1 atm, 72 h, pH 4.11 (measured). 2-phenyl imidazoline study Table 2, 0 mg/L row.
